calendar.matters:
Petition For Authority To Rent Trust?S Rental Property To Trustees Attorney
Off Calendar Jun-09-2025 Advanced From Aug-27-25 To Jul-14-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Authority To Rent Trust?S Rental Property To Trustees Attorney
Off Calendar Jul-28-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Rowena Navia, Esq., for and with Jordan Goldstein, Trustee.
Amy Harrington, Esq., for Neil Keeney, Beneficiary.
Andrew Schultz, Esq., in propria persona, interested Party.

At the court's query, counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the instant petition. At the conclusion of argument/discussion, the subject matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jul-23-2025 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Authority To Rent Trust?S Rental Property To Trustees Attorney
Off Calendar Jul-14-2025 Continued To Jul-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Schultz for and with Jordan Goldstein, Trustee (via Zoom).
Amy Harrington for Neil Keeney, Conservatee (via Zoom).

Mr. Schultz explained that he had filed the petition to ensure transparency and avoid any appearance of conflict, acknowledging the presumption of invalidity under Probate Code Sec. 16004. He confirmed he would not charge the trust for any related legal work and that a substitution of attorney had been executed to eliminate the conflict.

Ms. Navia confirmed her firm would address the issues raised in Ms. Harrington's report. Ms. Harrington, while not taking a position on the petition, raised concerns about whether renting the property was in the trust's best interest, especially given the availability of other investment options.

Ms. Goldstein described Mr. Keeney's personal attachment to the property and her desire to preserve it for the benefit of his daughter's special needs trust after his passing. The Court noted the importance of proper rent valuation and lease terms.

The Court continued the petition to July 28, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for the substitution of counsel to be filed, and for supplemental briefing addressing the concerns raised by Ms. Harrington.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
